Happy New Year, everyone!

Author: polyfractal
Content: Why does GF require COMP _and_ N+ or P+ layers? Would you ever use P+/N+ without it being an active region?

Author: bailey8889
Content: @BreakingTaps Not sure what you're asking. `COMP`, `N+`, `P+` are separate mask layers. Are you suggesting just using `N+` and `P+` and calculating the `COMP` mask layer from those?

Author: polyfractal
Content:
sorry, poorly worded. My understanding is that you need `COMP` + [`N+` or `P`] to define an active region. `COMP` by itself doesn't do anything, and I don't understand what `N+` / `P+` would equal if it wasn't also sitting on top of `COMP`.

Basically my question is why have `COMP` as a layer at all 🙂

Author: polyfractal
Content: oh I see, they are _actual_ physical mask layers. Implementation detail leaking to the user

Author: mithro_
Content: Masks is what I think they mean.

Author: 246tnt
Content: @BreakingTaps The N+/P+ region extend beyond COMP because those masks/process are not as precise.

Author: 246tnt
Content: So AFAIU `COMP` will define the STI region and the the implantation is done later and not as precise as the STI etch and so needs to extend beyond the active region to make sure it's covered and uniform and all that.

Author: chips4makers
Content: The N+/P+ has to enclose COMP by a certain amount but also they have to be a minimal distance from each other. So if you draw PCOMP and NCOMP only you need to generate the N+/P+ out of it with the right sizing plus filling of small spaces. This become quite complicated and even pretty difficult for two corner that are diagonallly too closo to each other. I prefer that PDKs allow me to draw COMP and N+/P+ separately and decide myself which N+/P+ regions to join into one.

Author: polyfractal
Content: ahh ok I see. cheers for the extra details 🙂
